mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% during rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 4.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during mid-cycle expansions, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 285.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in greater than 55% of periods following CPIAUCSL breaches above the 285.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate output and profit expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
